description: Matt Zenon Betz, a 42-year-old environmental site inspector, fell 3 meters from scaffolding and presents with severe left-sided chest pain, left thigh pain with visible deformity, and a scalp laceration. Vitals: HR 118 bpm, Systolic BP 95 mmHg. Injuries: scalp laceration, left-sided rib fractures (ribs 8-10), and a left femoral shaft fracture. As the attending emergency physician in GNU Health, document the trauma evaluation with the presenting vitals, record at least three distinct anatomical injury diagnoses using ICD-10 S-codes, order a standard trauma laboratory workup (at least three labs), prescribe acute analgesia, and schedule an orthopedic surgery follow-up appointment for 5 to 14 days from today.
